briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Flag denoting enabling of CSI.
Flag denoting disabling of CSI.
Max Lanes supported by CSI.
Max number of Virtual Channels.
Less no of lines recvd.
More no of lines recvd.
This typedef defines the different errors codes for Line Count status for a Virtual Channel when Frame End Generation is enabled.
Callback type for all interrupts defined.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XCSI2TX_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsi_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit to be set

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit to be reset

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift)
memberdef.name: XCsi2Tx_GetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit fields to be retrieved
is offset of the field in register

simplesect.para: Bit Field Value in u32 format

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift, u32 Value)
memberdef.name: XCsi2Tx_SetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit fields to be retrieved
is offset of the field in register
is to be set. Passed in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to start the soft reset process by setting the soft reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2 instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to stop the soft reset process by resetting the soft reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.This is done usually after Reset in Progress is 0.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to stop the packet processing by resetting Enable core bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to start the packet processing by setting Enable core bit in the Core Configuration Register.This is done after the configuration of active lanes, interrupt masks, etc.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the Core is enabled by checking the core enable bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - If the CSI core is enabled
0 - If the CSI core is disabled

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if lanes are in ulps mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - ulps mode enter
0 - ulps mode exit

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set lanes in ulps mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - ulps mode enter
0 - ulps mode exit

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the number of lanes configured in the IP.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Max number of lanes available in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the Pixel Mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0x0 - Single Pixel Mode 0x1 - Dual Pixel Mode 0x3 - Quad Pixel Mode

briefdescription.para: This function is to get the Line Synchronization packet Generation status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is to set the Line Synchronization packet Generation status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.
0 : Do not generate 1 : Generate

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the actual number of lanes being used by the IP to communicate with a CSI2 Tx.

detaileddescription.para: This is lesser or equal to Max lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Number of lanes being used in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the actual number of lanes to be used to communicate with a CSI2 Tx.

detaileddescription.para: This is lesser than or equal to Max lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.
is number of lanes to be made active

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if a reset is completed or is in progress.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - If the Soft reset operation is in progress
0 - If the Soft reset operation is completed

briefdescription.para: This function is used to disable the global interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is done after disabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the Short Packet Status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Number of Generic Short Packets can be safely written to Generic Short packet FIFO, before it goes full.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set value to Generic Short Packet Entry.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.
31:24 Reserved 23:16 Byte 1 of short packet 15:8 Byte 0 of short packet 7:6 VC value of short packet 5:0 Short packet data type

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the clock mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 0 - continuous clock mode
1 - non-continuous clock mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the clock mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 0 - Continuous Clock Mode
1 - Non-continuous Clock Mode

briefdescription.para: Look up the hardware configuration for a device instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device to lookup for

simplesect.para: The reference to the configuration record in the configuration table (in xcsi2tx_g.c) corresponding to the Device ID or if not found,a NULL pointer is returned.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XCsi2Tx instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific CSI.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS Initialization was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function will configure the core with proper number of Active Lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS On configuring the core.
XST_FAILURE if active lanes not set correctly

briefdescription.para: This function will enable/disable the IP Core to start processing.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.
will be used to indicate Enable or Disable action

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful core enable or disable
XST_FAILURE if core disable times out.

briefdescription.para: This function will do a reset of the IP.

detaileddescription.para: This will reset the values of all registers except Core Config and Protocol Config registers.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS On proper reset.
XST_FAILURE on timeout and core being stuck in reset

briefdescription.para: This function will get the short packet received in the FIFO from the Generic Short Packet Register and fill up the structure passed from caller.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the active lanes parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is the lane count to check if valid.

listitem.para: 1 if specified Active Lanes is valid.
0 otherwise, if the Active Lanes specified isn't valid as per spec and design.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: This is to be called before starting the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is valid line count for the Virtual channel.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.
XST_FAILURE in case the core is already running.
XST_SUCCESS otherwise

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is pointer to variable to be filled with line count for the Virtual channel

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.
XST_SUCCESS otherwise

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test checks if the LaneCount present in register matches the one from the generated file.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if self-test was successful
XST_FAILURE if the read value was not equal to _g.c file

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the CSI2 Tx core.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from the Interrupt Status register, determines the source of the interrupts and calls the respective callbacks for the interrupts that are enabled in Interrupt Enable register, and finally clears the interrupts.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this core is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCsi2Tx_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2Tx core instance.

simplesect.para: Interrupt should be enabled to execute interrupt handler.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ---------------------------- -------------------------------------------- (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_WRG_LANE) IncorrectLaneCallBack (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_GSPFIFO_FULL) GSPFIFOCallBack (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_ULPS) DPhyUlpsCallBack (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_LINEBUF_FULL) LineBufferCallBack (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_WRG_DATATYPE) WrgDataTypeCallBack (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_UNDERRUN_PIXEL) UnderrunPixelCallBack (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_LCERRVC0) LineCountErrVC0 (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_LCERRVC1) LineCountErrVC1 (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_LCERRVC2) LineCountErrVC2 (XCSI2TX_HANDLER_LCERRVC3) LineCountErrVC3

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the Callbackfunc

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS when handler is installed.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the interrupt mask set (enabled) in the CSI2 Tx core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on

simplesect.para: Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt in Interrupt enable register

briefdescription.para: This function will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: This function will disable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: This function will get the list of interrupts pending in the Interrupt Status Register of the CSI2 Tx core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on

simplesect.para: Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt in Interrupt Status register

briefdescription.para: This function will clear the interrupts set in the Interrupt Status Register of the CSI2 Tx core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on
is Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt to be cleared in the Interrupt Status register
